description: A teen male instructor in an ocean-print rash guard supports a toddler girl in a pink patterned suit and purple goggles. Toys and beach ball in the background give the lobby its playful, kid-friendly feel.
Same pair now working with a watermelon-patterned dive ball, the girl reaching for it. Combines play and skill-building beautifully.
Same coach and girl share a smile as she tries to roll the watermelon float in the water. Pure 'engaged toddler' lesson joy.
The pink-goggled girl laughs while the coach supports her from behind — the watermelon float just out of frame. Captures the energy of class momentum.
Same pair: the girl smiles openly while the coach watches, hand gently resting on her back. A kind, secure 'coaching with care' moment.
The ocean-print coach supports the girl as she practices a prone-float with her face down, hair just visible at the water surface. Demonstrates the 'controlled submersion' skill.
The girl clutches the watermelon dive ball with both hands, laughing up at her smiling coach in the pool. Showcases 'gripping the float' as a fun mini-milestone.
Vertical frame of the smiling coach and goggled girl holding the watermelon ball between them. The coach's proud smile reads as 'she's got it.'
The coach engages a different small student in orange goggles — face-to-face at water level — with a bold, curious baby expression. A strong 'focused coaching' snapshot.
The same coach smiles as another small student (goggles on, braided hair) swims alongside him. Captures a 'ready to go' pre-drill moment.

Teen male instructor in a 'hard things' blue long-sleeve supports a toddler girl in a hot-pink suit and purple goggles with a teal kickboard as she practices a streamline glide. Solid 'first glide' documentation.
Same pair — the girl clings to the kickboard while the coach holds her side. The splashes of water give the shot a dynamic, real-class feel.
Angled shot with the girl lying prone on the teal kickboard, goggles fixed on her target. Shows advanced-beginner kickboard work.
The 'hard things' coach and goggled girl focus on a prone glide exercise with the teal kickboard extended forward. Captures determined skill-building.
Back-angle view of the 'COACH' tee and kickboard drill — the goggled girl glides forward, face down, hands gripping the board. Good for an 'advanced-beginner' program illustration.
Close-up of the same girl pulling herself forward with the teal board, the male coach supporting from behind. Crisp 'momentum building' image.
Male coach in a powder-blue rash guard works a small red-haired toddler boy practicing a prone kick with a teal kickboard. Another swimmer in goggles is blurred in the background.
Same coach and red-haired boy continue the kickboard drill, the coach extending his arm out. Captures the 'now try by yourself' moment.
The teal-kickboard drill continues with the red-haired boy kicking and the coach easing back. Shows the trust-transfer that defines good coaching.
The blue-sleeved coach smiles at the red-haired toddler who clutches the teal kickboard. The bright smile + eye contact makes it a great social-post image.
